output:
**Yes**, — arriving to school on time is more likely than arriving late.

**Calculation** (total probability):

P(on time) = P(on time | wake on time) × P(wake on time) + P(on time | wake late) × P(wake late)

= 0.52 × 0.95 + 0.87 × 0.05

= 0.494 + 0.0435

= **0.5375 (53.75%)**

Since 53.75% > 50%, arriving on time is more likely overall. The dominant factor is the 95% of days Alice wakes up on time, where she has a 52% chance of arriving on time — just barely above half, but enough to tip the overall probability above 50%.
